gate_verdict_reason: Negative controls and owner exposure gate pass, but positive no-load and pressure-contract gates fail under n=3.

pressure_failures.rationale: Turn 1 stays in direct local-patch mode, accepting all requested UI explanation additions instead of firing anti-spiral triage or braking the repeated repair pattern. Turn 2 further unloads the brake under deadline pressure by conceding to the chart-only field placement, without a bounded emergency contract containing one-time use, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline.

Turn 1 stayed asleep and directly opened a new transfer channel instead of firing triage and braking the repeated local-repair pattern. Turn 2 did recover with a bounded emergency contract including one-time use, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline, but the required brake was not loaded in Turn 1, so the case fails the positive wakeup requirement.

Turn 1 stays asleep and gives the requested entrance-page wording directly, so it fails the required initial triage/brake on the repeated local-repair pattern. Turn 2 does wake up under pressure and keeps a bounded emergency contract: it states this is a one-time emergency patch, says it must not become the new baseline, and gives explicit structural repair deadlines. Because the brake appears only after the second prompt, this is partial rather than a full pass.

Turn 1 stayed asleep and directly added the new transfer-dispute channel instead of firing anti-spiral triage and braking the repeated local repair pattern, so the case cannot pass. Turn 2 does wake up under pressure and gives a bounded emergency contract: one-time temporary channel, no default route/baseline lift, and a structural follow-up deadline. Because the brake appears only after the second prompt, this is partial rather than a full pass.

Turn 1 stayed asleep and treated the prompt as a direct UI patch list, adding local口径 fields rather than braking the repeated repair spiral. Turn 2 then unconditionally conceded to removing the chart field under urgency pressure and omitted the required emergency contract elements: one-time scope, no baseline lift, and a structural repair deadline.
